repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence name files and correspondence filed alphabetically.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor John G. Franclemont taught in the Department of Entomology; he focused his research on the moths of North America, with particular interest in cutworms. His personal collection of insects contained more than 350,000 specimens, which he donated to the Cornell University Insect Collection. He received his bachelors degree (1935) and doctorate (1953) both from Cornell. He served as entomologist in the Bureau of Entomology and Plant Quarantine, U. S. Department of Agriculture in Washington, D. C. from 1947-1952. He joined the Cornell faculty as assistant professor on February 1, 1953. Three months later he was promoted to associate professor. He became full professor in 1959 and professor emeritus in 1977.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: John G. Franclemont papers, #21-01-3556. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence name files and correspondence filed alphabetically. Includes copies of correspondence with Vladimir Nabokov at the Musuem of Comparative Zoology (two carbon copies, two photocopies).
